title: Hay-on-Wye & Llanthony Abbey (Black Mountains)

main_route.excerpt: On to Hay where we met up in the campsite with Thomas, Naomi + Austin.

excursions.label: Drive to Llanthony Abbey via Capel-y-Ffin through the Black Mountains

stops.excerpt: On the way we stopped at Capel-y-Ffin + Eve recognized the gill tombstones that Simon had seen on on earlier trip.
then a long drive to Llanthony Abbey, through the Black Forest mountains, a very twisty road through narrow lanes

boundary_flags.issue: boundary_mixed: end of spread blends into the next trip; only Saturday June 7th is dated on this spread, so the trip likely spans more days than captured here.
boundary_flags.excerpt: Eve closed wisely all the windows. So we were warm in the

summary: A short June 2008 camping trip to Hay-on-Wye, where the family met Thomas, Naomi and Austin at the campsite, browsed the bookshops and looked at maps. From the Hay base they made a day drive through the Black Mountains, stopping at Capel-y-Ffin (where Eve recognised the Eric Gill tombstones) and on to Llanthony Abbey, returning to camp to eat at the Three Tuns in Hay.
extraction_notes: Single spread (B_0137); only Saturday June 7th is dated (confirmed a Saturday in 2008). The route-sketch image on B_0137_R is a drawing of the old iron bridge (zigzag with dates '1835-1956'), not a route-order sketch (sketch_desc confirms no labels/arrows), so narrative order was used. 'Black Forest mountains' in the diary is an error for the Black Mountains in Wales, confirmed by the page caption and places list; the whole trip is in Wales/GB with no sea crossing. The Llanthony drive (via Capel-y-Ffin) is recorded as an excursion because the family 'returned to the campsite' in Hay the same day. spread_range only spans B_0137; mixed_end flagged as the spread blends into the following trip.
